item.title: Checking Python files with Vim
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Vim is an exôÙcelôÙlent edôÙiôÙtor, and masôÙterôÙing it leads to more proôÙducôÙtivôÙiôÙty. Even though is very exôÙtenôÙsiôÙble and alôÙlows to be conôÙfigôÙured by many plug-ins I rather keep it as simôÙple as posôÙsiôÙble, tryôÙing not to use many plug-ins (neiôÙther packôÙagers like VunôÙdle, etc.).

HowôÙevôÙer, I do make use of an exôÙtenôÙsion that checks Python files for erôÙrors, PEP8, aôÙmong othôÙer things: flake8. BeôÙcause I do not use plug-in platôÙforms for Vim, I inôÙstall just this one manôÙuôÙalôÙly, by makôÙing the comôÙmand flake8 availôÙable sysôÙtem-wide [1].

Then the inôÙstalôÙlaôÙtion is as simôÙple as downôÙloadôÙing the project and coyôÙing the files inôÙto the ~/.vim/ftôÙôÙplugôÙin/python diôÙrecôÙtoôÙry. Make sure you have the folôÙlowôÙing line added on your ~/.vimôÙrc:

filetype plugin indent on

The feaôÙtures I use are mainôÙly the synôÙtax and PEP-8 comôÙpliôÙance checkôÙerôÙs. It can alôÙso warn you about unôÙused imôÙportôÙs, and cyôÙcloôÙmatôÙic comôÙplexôÙiôÙty.

It is useôÙful beôÙcause things like PEP-8 comôÙpliôÙance help to have a good code qualôÙiôÙty, and thereôÙfore a more readôÙable and mainôÙtainôÙable code base, speôÙcialôÙly on large projects with lots of files and modôÙules.

That's alôÙl. For more deôÙtails and othôÙer conôÙfigôÙuôÙraôÙtion tips checkôÙout my Vim setôÙup.

[1]Another option would be to install it on your virtual environment, but then you have to make sure to install it once per project. It is actually better, because you are not using the global system environment, but for packages like this, it should not be an issue, it's your choice.

item.title: Alpine email client
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
Alpine is a softôÙware I defôÙiôÙniteôÙly recôÙomôÙmend for those who like minôÙiôÙmalôÙisôÙtic apôÙpliôÙcaôÙtionôÙs that go straight to the point in orôÙder to get the things done. It's an email clienôÙt, with no GUI, which alôÙlows me to read emails betôÙter than by means of the web page [1] withôÙout any disôÙtracôÙtionôÙs, foôÙcusôÙing on what is just imôÙporôÙtanôÙt. It is alôÙso great that shows onôÙly the text (the relôÙeôÙvant part of the email) and it can folôÙlow the links by launchôÙing the deôÙfault browsôÙer.

It is simôÙple, fast and perôÙfectôÙly suitôÙable for checkôÙing my old inôÙbox with weekôÙly lists upôÙdates. It's inôÙherôÙitôÙed from an old proôÙjecôÙt, called pine, and in FeôÙdoôÙra it can be inôÙstalled by:

sudo dnf install alpine

Then you just need to conôÙfigôÙure the email acôÙcount setôÙtings (one last time to visôÙit the page, but that would be the last :-) and you're ready.

[1]Not for gmail but from another older account I have
